text:
This is great!

After that episode, I dug out an old Snow Leopard-era iMac and put Kid Pix on it. Been having a blast with that — and my 6yo is doing so much more with it than any of the iPad drawing apps we've tried. (As soon as she gets a little further on reading, I'm gonna try learning etoys with her)

So maybe the computer feeling is just nostalgia, because software was truly better back then.

text: Feeling the architecture astronaut, "Today, let's work on a system for reviewing the system that tunes the system that monitors the QA system."

elements.text: Feeling the architecture astronaut, "Today, let's work on a system for reviewing the system that tunes the system that monitors the QA system."

text: Yes, but do you have polecats?

elements.text: Yes, but do you have polecats?

text: I don't use separate agents for this — that would be madness.  Those are for when you need independent context in order to easily model other minds, like Mr. Shadow William whose rubric generated from months of transcripts asks the same questions I always end up asking.
